preamble: The shift from a vowel to [h] is typically a result of extreme devoicing. In rapid or whispered speech, an unstressed [a] can lose its vocal fold vibration entirely, leaving only the friction of air passing through the glottis.

examples.note: In rapid speech, unstressed vowels can become devoiced, particularly in Northern dialects.

sources: Arvaniti, A. (2007). Greek phonetics: The state of the art. Journal of Greek Linguistics, 8, 97–208.
